words.note: ledger-bearer; one entered in the public record
cf. the Peace of the Scale
ta (to) + fen (after/end, cf fenir): up to the end of
nav (not) + mel (some): none of the two (nav+ven would collapse to naven = empty)
from aur (light): in the light, openly
from kael (trade): to gain by labour
from kelv- (grasp, cf kelvir 'to hold'), forceful: to seize
nav + sol (pact): to form a counter-pact
na- (not, before v) + velth ('to write') + -ir: to un-write, strike from the record
dur (metal/forge) + dorir (to make/build): to forge, produce
from thaevir (to grow): to make grow
dhar- (break, cf dharvir) + -un: an incurred break
kal- (fore/upper, cf kaln 'head') + -is: the fore of the head
ur (age) + -an: an age-cohort, those of one age
dael (to give) + -un: a thing given in
vesh (belief, cf veshael) + -il: a held principle
from fen (end, cf fenir): of the end
vel- (collective) + val- (people, cf valan/velvalan) + -en: of the people
na- (not, before v) + velven (bound): not held in check
na- (not, before v) + vels- (record, cf velsir) + -en: not entered in the record
Valdoria + -en (the language's own name is Vethrin)
proper name: gilet- (silver, cf gileth) + vel (clasp/bond)
arel- (again) + sol- (pact, cf solvir "to ally"): to re-form a bond
causative of lhael (to see): to make-see
from morv- (decay); distinct from morvir "to die"
from gorn (rock): to crush to grit
noun of arsolvir: a re-formed bond
